definition: Near-round or oval object geometry, often consistent with a theca, medallion, or round reliquary housing.
research_use: Prioritize for theca-front and reverse review.
next_step: Capture close inscription, seal/thread detail, and reverse if visible.

definition: Near-square or vertical object with lower fill ratio suggesting openwork, cross arms, negative space, or shaped reliquary design.
research_use: Narrow likely cross-shaped or ornate reliquary objects.
next_step: Manual shape review; inspect arms, loop, theca placement, and mount.

definition: Wide horizontal object likely to be a brass label, paper strip, or horizontal certificate fragment.
research_use: Prioritize for transcription workflow rather than relic-object review.
next_step: Manual transcription or OCR; link to record_id.

definition: Tall narrow object that may be a vertical card, certificate, mount, or long reliquary object.
research_use: Flag for document/certificate or tall-object review.
next_step: Inspect for text, seal, fold, border, or mounted relic.

definition: Rectangular high-fill object likely to be a card, certificate, label field, case detail, or flat mount.
research_use: Route toward document, label, or mount review.
next_step: Check for text, borders, signatures, seal traces, and catalog linkage.

definition: Bounded but irregular object that may include a theca, reliquary object, seal, mixed mount, or background artifact.
research_use: Queue for human review where geometry is ambiguous.
next_step: Retake on clean background if needed; inspect manually.

definition: Very small detected object in pixel dimensions.
research_use: Flag possible fragment, loose material, dust, or image noise.
next_step: Retake with macro focus and scale card; do not infer relic class.

definition: Potential seal or wax impression; currently reserved for future color/shape refinement.
research_use: Useful for provenance and authentication-document review once implemented.
next_step: Capture reverse/seal close-up and document color/texture.

definition: Object does not fit current visual taxonomy.
